cards.back: A digital circuit that takes three one-bit inputs (A, B, C_in) and produces a one-bit output (S) and a carry output (C_out) for a four-bit system.

cards.front: What is a Full Adder equation?
cards.back: A full adder equation is given by S = A ⊕ B ⊕ C_in, C_out = (A ∧ B) ∨ (A ∧ C_in) ∨ (B ∧ C_in)

cards.front: How to design a 4-bit Full Adder?
cards.back: A 4-bit Full Adder can be designed using 16 2-input Full Adders, one for each bit position.

cards.front: What is a Full Adder Subtractor?
cards.back: A digital circuit that takes three one-bit inputs (A, B, C_in) and produces a one-bit output (S) and a carry output (C_out) for a four-bit system, where S = A ⊕ B ⊕ C_in and C_out = (A ∧ B) ∨ (A ∧ C_in) ∨ (B ∧ C_in)

cards.front: How to design a 4-bit Full Adder Subtractor?
cards.back: A 4-bit Full Adder Subtractor can be designed using 16 2-input Full Adders and 16 2-input NOT gates, one for each bit position.
